name: Brugada syndrome
phenotype: Sudden cardiac death risk in young adults; ECG shows coved or saddleback ST elevation in V1-V3 with right bundle branch block; arrhythmic syncope; family history of unexplained sudden death.

name: Long QT syndrome
phenotype: Prolonged QT interval on ECG; syncope or seizures triggered by exercise, emotion, or auditory stimuli; risk of torsades de pointes; congenital or drug-induced.

name: Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
phenotype: Asymmetric left ventricular hypertrophy; exertional dyspnea; angina; syncope; sudden death in athletes; outflow tract obstruction murmur.

name: Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy
phenotype: Right ventricular dysfunction; ventricular arrhythmias of left bundle branch block morphology; epsilon waves on ECG; exercise-induced palpitations or syncope.

name: POTS (postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome)
phenotype: Heart-rate increase >=30 bpm within 10 minutes of standing without orthostatic hypotension; lightheadedness; fatigue; brain fog; symptoms worse with heat or dehydration.

name: Wolff-Parkinson-White syndrome
phenotype: Short PR interval; delta wave on ECG; supraventricular tachycardia episodes; pre-excitation via accessory pathway.

name: Cardiac amyloidosis
phenotype: Restrictive cardiomyopathy; low ECG voltages with paradoxical LV hypertrophy on echo; carpal tunnel; heart failure with preserved ejection fraction; progressive dyspnea.

name: Pulmonary embolism
phenotype: Sudden dyspnea; pleuritic chest pain; tachycardia; hypoxemia; S1Q3T3 pattern on ECG; risk after immobilization, surgery, or pregnancy.

name: Idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis
phenotype: Progressive exertional dyspnea; dry cough; bibasilar inspiratory crackles; finger clubbing; restrictive lung function; reticular opacities at lung bases.

name: Cystic fibrosis
phenotype: Chronic productive cough; recurrent pulmonary infections; bronchiectasis; pancreatic insufficiency; salty sweat; failure to thrive in children.

name: Sleep apnea (obstructive)
phenotype: Loud snoring; witnessed apneas; daytime somnolence; morning headaches; nocturnal hypoxemia; fragmented sleep architecture.

name: Asthma exacerbation
phenotype: Wheezing; prolonged expiratory phase; accessory muscle use; tachypnea; hypoxia; reduced peak expiratory flow; trigger exposure (allergens, exercise, infection).

name: Parkinson disease
phenotype: Resting tremor; bradykinesia; rigidity; postural instability; shuffling gait; reduced arm swing; micrographia; voice hypophonia.

name: Essential tremor
phenotype: Action tremor of upper extremities; bilateral; worse with sustained posture or movement; relieved by alcohol; family history.

name: Multiple sclerosis
phenotype: Relapsing-remitting neurological deficits; optic neuritis; sensory disturbances; ataxia; fatigue; gait imbalance; symptoms worse with heat (Uhthoff phenomenon).

name: Stroke (ischemic)
phenotype: Acute focal neurologic deficit; facial droop; arm weakness; speech disturbance; gait asymmetry; risk factors include atrial fibrillation, hypertension, diabetes.

name: Preeclampsia
phenotype: New-onset hypertension >=140/90 after 20 weeks gestation; proteinuria; edema; headache; visual disturbances; right upper quadrant pain; thrombocytopenia.

name: Eclampsia
phenotype: Preeclampsia complicated by generalized tonic-clonic seizures; severe hypertension; headache; visual disturbances; abdominal pain.

name: Placental abruption
phenotype: Sudden vaginal bleeding; severe abdominal pain; rigid uterus; non-reassuring fetal heart rate; risk after trauma, hypertension, or cocaine use.

name: Chorioamnionitis
phenotype: Maternal fever; uterine tenderness; foul-smelling discharge; fetal tachycardia; maternal tachycardia; leukocytosis; risk after prolonged rupture of membranes.

name: Preterm labor
phenotype: Regular uterine contractions before 37 weeks gestation; cervical change; possible spotting; back pain; pelvic pressure; EHG signal abnormalities.

name: Fetal hypoxia
phenotype: Late or variable decelerations on CTG; reduced beat-to-beat variability; bradycardia <=110 bpm sustained; tachycardia >=160 bpm; absence of accelerations; non-reassuring Dawes-Redman criteria.

name: Diabetic retinopathy
phenotype: Microaneurysms; dot-blot hemorrhages; hard exudates; cotton wool spots; neovascularization; macular edema; long-standing diabetes mellitus.

name: Glaucoma (primary open-angle)
phenotype: Elevated intraocular pressure; optic disc cupping; peripheral visual field loss; gradual painless vision loss; family history.

name: Age-related macular degeneration
phenotype: Central vision loss; metamorphopsia; drusen on fundus; pigmentary changes; geographic atrophy (dry) or choroidal neovascularization (wet); age >=50.

name: Hypertensive retinopathy
phenotype: Arteriovenous nicking; copper or silver wiring; flame hemorrhages; cotton wool spots; papilledema in severe cases; chronic uncontrolled hypertension.

name: Melanoma (cutaneous)
phenotype: Asymmetric pigmented lesion; irregular borders; color variegation; diameter >=6mm; evolving size or shape; history of sun exposure or atypical nevi.

name: Psoriasis
phenotype: Well-demarcated erythematous plaques with silvery scale; extensor surfaces, scalp, nails; pruritus; possible joint involvement (psoriatic arthritis).

name: Atopic dermatitis
phenotype: Pruritic erythematous patches; flexural distribution; xerosis; lichenification with chronicity; personal or family history of atopy.

name: Sepsis
phenotype: Fever or hypothermia; tachycardia >90; tachypnea >20; leukocytosis or leukopenia; hypotension; altered mental status; suspected infection source; lactate elevation.

name: Hyperthyroidism
phenotype: Tachycardia; tremor; weight loss; heat intolerance; anxiety; diarrhea; goiter; exophthalmos; atrial fibrillation in older patients.

name: Pheochromocytoma
phenotype: Episodic hypertension; headaches; palpitations; diaphoresis; pallor; orthostatic hypotension; symptom triggers include exertion, anesthesia, or tyramine-rich foods.
